title: Pan-Seared Japanese Veal Kushikatsu
description: This keto-friendly dish is a variation of Japanese Kushikatsu, a dish originating from Chubu region. Veal is coated in a light batter, deep-fried, then pan-seared for a crispy exterior and tender interior.
instructions: Dredge the veal cubes in coconut flour, shaking off excess.
Dip the veal cubes in beaten eggs.
Coat the veal cubes with a light batter made of 1 cup of coconut flour and 1 cup of water.
Heat sesame oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Deep-fry the veal cubes until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the veal cubes from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Add a small amount of sesame oil to the pan.
Pan-sear the veal cubes until crispy and golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Serve the veal cubes hot with lacto-fermented daikon on the side.
